headline: Student Transportation Costs Burden 180,000 Rural Families
context: Inadequate school bus services force rural families to spend up to 20% of income on student transportation.

cards.big: 180,000 rural families spend over RM200 monthly on school transportation in 2025
cards.sub: Government school bus subsidies cover only 42% of eligible rural routes

cards.big: Average rural student commute time is 48 minutes each way versus 18 minutes in cities
cards.sub: Long commutes reduce study time and increase fatigue among rural students

cards.big: APAD licensed only 12,400 school buses nationally but demand requires an estimated 18,000
cards.sub: Shortfall forces families to use unlicensed transport or personal vehicles

cards.big: India's Samagra Shiksha provides free transport for students living beyond 1km from school
cards.sub: Universal transport subsidy removes distance as a barrier to school attendance

cards.big: Distance should not determine whether a child receives an education in a modern nation
Should all rural students receive free government-provided school transportation
